accept: act show <id> in text mode renders 'description:' (indented, possibly truncated to N lines with a … marker for very long descriptions) when present and non-empty.
act show <id> in text mode renders 'commit_marker:' on a clean (non-tombstoned) issue.
Long descriptions truncate at a defined boundary (e.g. 80 chars * 5 lines = 400 chars then '…'); --include-ops or a new --full flag prints the entire description without truncation.
payload.description: Discovered 2026-05-10 by UX-eval agent. 'act show <id>' (no --json) prints id, title, status, priority, type, created_at, dep lines. The description is in the --json output but invisible in human-readable mode. Same with commit_marker, which CLAUDE.md and act help promise the system surfaces. Agents reach for 'show --json | jq' for routine inspection — friction. Multi-line descriptions (e.g. agent-filed bug repros) are completely invisible in the default surface.
payload.title: act show text mode hides description and commit_marker
